title: Session 02 - Nouns Repeat Practice
subject: english
description: Repeat practice for nouns, articles, countability, noun phrases and natural work-English corrections.

questions.prompt: Choose the most natural sentence.
options: I work in food service sector in UK.
I work in the food service sector in the UK.
I work in a food service sector in the UK.
questions.answer: I work in the food service sector in the UK.
questions.explanation: Use 'the food service sector' and 'the UK'.

questions.prompt: Complete: Most of my ____ are Turkish restaurants.
questions.explanation: Use the plural noun after 'Most of my' when speaking about many customers.

questions.prompt: Complete: We had two late ____ this week. (delivery)
questions.explanation: Delivery becomes deliveries.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct article: I want to create ____ regular ordering system.
questions.explanation: System is a singular countable noun, so it needs an article.

questions.prompt: Choose the natural sentence.
options: I need an information about the order.
I need some information about the order.
I need many information about the order.
questions.answer: I need some information about the order.
questions.explanation: Information is uncountable.

questions.prompt: Choose the most natural business collocation.
options: The customer gave an order to me.
The customer placed an order with me.
The customer made order on me.
questions.answer: The customer placed an order with me.
questions.explanation: Use 'place an order with someone'.

questions.prompt: In this sentence, what is the direct object? The customer placed an order.
questions.explanation: Ask: placed what?

questions.prompt: In this sentence, what is the subject? The delivery arrived late.
questions.explanation: Ask: what arrived late?

questions.prompt: Choose the correct possessive phrase.
questions.explanation: Use apostrophe + s for most singular possessive nouns.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct phrase for many restaurants.
questions.explanation: A regular plural noun ending in s takes only the apostrophe.

questions.prompt: Choose the general statement.
questions.explanation: Use no article for general plural nouns.

questions.prompt: Choose the general statement.
questions.explanation: Quality is usually uncountable when used generally.

questions.prompt: Complete the noun phrase: product ____
questions.explanation: Product quality is a natural compound noun phrase.

questions.prompt: Complete the noun phrase: price ____
questions.explanation: Price list is the natural noun phrase.

questions.prompt: Correct this sentence: Customer asked new price list.
questions.answer: The customer asked for the new price list.
questions.explanation: Use 'the customer', 'asked for', and 'the new price list'.

questions.prompt: Correct this sentence: We had problem with delivery.
questions.answer: We had a problem with the delivery.
questions.explanation: Problem is singular countable; delivery is specific here.

questions.prompt: Correct this sentence: My customers mostly Turkish restaurants.
questions.answer: Most of my customers are Turkish restaurants.
questions.explanation: The sentence needs a complete subject phrase and the verb 'are'.

questions.prompt: Correct this sentence: I have salary and commissions on monthly payments.
questions.answer: I earn a salary and commission based on monthly payments.
questions.explanation: Use the natural payment collocations: earn a salary, earn commission, based on.

questions.prompt: Write one natural sentence using: a regular weekly order.
questions.answer: A good customer places a regular weekly order with me.
questions.explanation: Your own sentence can also be correct if the noun phrase is natural.

questions.prompt: Write one natural sentence using: product quality.
questions.answer: Product quality is very important for restaurant customers.
questions.explanation: Your own sentence can also be correct if the noun phrase is natural.
